key_insight: PragReST teaches LLMs pragmatic inference by self-generating counterfactual reasoning traces and reinforcing them, reducing the tendency to default to literal interpretations.
engineering_relevance: The framework offers a data-efficient way to improve language understanding in systems that must infer implied meaning, without requiring human-labeled pragmatic training data or a stronger teacher model.
method: Constructs pragmatic QA data automatically from the model itself.
Generates counterfactual reasoning traces that contrast observed utterances with plausible alternatives.
Uses supervised fine-tuning plus reinforcement learning to internalize the counterfactual reasoning policy.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on four pragmatic benchmarks (PragMega, Ludwig, MetoQA, AltPrag), PragReST outperformed backbones and non-counterfactual variants, improving accuracy by 5.37 and 5.50 percentage points for Qwen3-8B and Qwen3-14B on accuracy-based tasks.
watch_item: The gains depend on high-quality counterfactual generation, so noisy or weakly grounded synthetic traces could limit robustness in new domains.
paper_title: PragReST: Self-Reinforcing Counterfactual Reasoning for Pragmatic Language Understanding
